clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' first, then stop it cleanly with 't' — no vowel before or between ('stop', not 'estop').
common_errors: Adding a vowel before: 'estop' (common for Spanish and Portuguese speakers)
practice_sentences: Stop at the station and stay still.
The student started studying statistics last September.

clusters.articulation_tip: Start the 's' hiss with your lips already closing for 'p', then release the 'p' without a vowel in between.
common_errors: Aspirating the 'p' too strongly (after 's' it should be soft)
practice_sentences: Speak Spanish at the sports space.
The spider spun a special spiral web.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' while the back of your tongue rises for 'k', then release straight into the vowel.
practice_sentences: The school skating club skipped the skills test.
The scared skier scanned the sky.

clusters.articulation_tip: Start the 's' hiss, stop it crisply with 't', and release straight into 'r' — three sounds, no vowels between them.
practice_sentences: The street is straight and strong.
Strangers under stress struggled with the strict structure.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss 's', close the lips for 'p', and release directly into 'r' with rounded lips — one smooth burst.
practice_sentences: In spring, sprinkle water on the sprouts.
The sprinter sprained his ankle while sprinting.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss 's', close the lips for 'p', and release directly into 'l' with the tongue tip on the ridge behind your teeth.
practice_sentences: The splendid splash split the silence.
Don't splatter paint when you split the boards.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss 's', raise the back of the tongue for 'k', and release directly into 'r' — keep the three sounds tight together.
practice_sentences: Scroll the screen and read the script.
The scared cat scratched and screamed.

clusters.articulation_tip: Make the 'sh' sound with rounded lips and glide straight into 'r' without any vowel — the lips stay rounded for both.
practice_sentences: The shrimp shrank in the shrinking pond.
She shrugged and shredded the paper near the shrub.

clusters.articulation_tip: Touch your tongue tip between your teeth for 'th', then pull it back quickly into 'r' — the tongue moves from teeth to curled-back in one motion.
practice_sentences: Throw three threads through the hole.
The thrilling throne speech threatened no one.

clusters.articulation_tip: Place the tongue for 'd' and release it directly into 'r' — in fast speech it sounds almost like 'jr' ('drive' ≈ 'jrive'), which is normal.
practice_sentences: Don't drink and drive.
The dragon dreamed of drumming in the dry desert.

clusters.articulation_tip: Place the tongue for 't' and release it directly into 'r' — in fast speech it sounds almost like 'chr' ('try' ≈ 'chry'), which is normal.
practice_sentences: Try to travel by train.
The truck driver tracked the trail through the trees.

clusters.articulation_tip: Press the back of the tongue for 'g' and release straight into 'r' — keep the voice on through both sounds.
practice_sentences: The green grass grows on the ground.
Grandma's group graduated with great grades.

clusters.articulation_tip: Press the back of the tongue for 'k' and release straight into 'r' with a small puff of air.
practice_sentences: The crazy crowd crossed the crosswalk.
The creative critic criticized the cream-colored crown.

clusters.articulation_tip: Close the lips for 'p' and release them straight into 'l' with the tongue tip already on the ridge behind your teeth.
practice_sentences: Please place the plant on the plate.
We planned plenty of plays at the playground.

clusters.articulation_tip: Close the lips for 'b' (voice on) and release them straight into 'l' — no vowel between.
practice_sentences: The black and blue blanket blocked the breeze.
Don't blame the blind man for the blank page.

clusters.articulation_tip: Touch your upper teeth to your lower lip for 'f' and release the air straight into 'l'.
practice_sentences: The flag flew over the flat floor.
Flowers flowed along the flooded field.

clusters.articulation_tip: Press the back of the tongue for 'k' and release straight into 'l' with the tongue tip on the ridge.
practice_sentences: Clean the classroom clock.
The climbing club closed under clear clouds.

clusters.articulation_tip: Press the back of the tongue for 'g' (voice on) and release straight into 'l'.
practice_sentences: The glass globe glowed gladly.
She glanced at the glittering glue on her gloves.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' and slide the tongue tip up to the ridge for 'l' without stopping the airflow.
practice_sentences: The sleepy sloth slid slowly down the slope.
Slice the bread slowly so it doesn't slip.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' and close the lips into 'm', letting the sound move into the nose — keep it one smooth motion.
practice_sentences: The small smart boy smiled.
The smooth smoke smelled of smoked salmon.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' with the tongue tip near the ridge, then seal it there for 'n' and let the sound exit through the nose.
practice_sentences: The snake snapped at the snack in the snow.
His sneezing and snoring scared the snail.

clusters.articulation_tip: Hiss the 's' while rounding your lips for 'w', then release into the vowel — the lips round during the 's'.
practice_sentences: The swan swam swiftly in the sweet water.
Switch off the light and swing the sweater over your shoulder.
